note: Fix the auto-split capsule send failure report rendering as raw HTML in the wallet's send result panel. The report builder in autoSplitCapsuleSend produces a styled red/green block containing per-chunk txids and an aggregate status; both branches funneled through showSuccess (innerHTML) for the success case and showError (textContent) for the failure case. The failure path therefore escaped the markup, so a partial-failure report rendered to the user as a literal '<div style=...>Auto-split: 1 / 3 transactions broadcast...' string instead of the styled panel. Adds a sibling helper showErrorHtml(elId, html) that mirrors showSuccess (innerHTML + remove hidden) but applies the red border, and routes only the auto-split failure call site through it. The other 19 showError call sites that pass plain text (recipient invalid, 2FA invalid, send/sendmany failed, RPC errors, address book block, policy violation, in-flight guard) keep their textContent escaping unchanged so any user- or network-supplied string in those messages cannot inject markup. The single field inside the auto-split report that is not under our control — failure.message, which may originate from a node RPC error returned by sendrawtransaction — is escaped inline at the splice site (& < > only) before being concatenated into the trusted HTML block. NO consensus, NO RPC schema, NO node, NO miner, NO transaction wire format. Pure wallet UI fix.
